subcodes.title: Words spoken at Rue St. Didier 30 in Paris, 1913-02-20
subcodes.first_line: Lycurgus was well-versed in laws and he wished to treat his subjects justly. As he looked over the Spartans, he observed that they were divided into three classes… One day in the company of the Governor
subcodes.first_line_orig: در ایام لکورغه اولاد خود را بقانون و ترتیب مخصوصی تربیت می‌کردند اولاً در وقت ازدواج
subcodes.subjects: @useful occupation; acquisition of a craft; trade; profession, @monasticism; asceticism, @serving humanity; working for progress, betterment of the world
